baseVersion: v1 complete as of S74 on branch ralph/luminary-v1

v2Philosophy.theme: The system becomes an agent that reasons about HOW to answer, not a pipeline that always retrieves and generates.
principles: Hierarchical knowledge: ingestion builds a three-level summary pyramid — section → document → library. Every level is pre-computed and stored. Nothing expensive is ever recomputed when inputs have not changed (Core Belief #18).

Agentic chat: the QA pipeline is a LangGraph graph, not a linear function. A classifier node decides the retrieval strategy before any retrieval happens. The graph is the explicit, versioned contract for how questions are answered.

Performance budget: ingestion of a large book (Iliad, ~3300 chunks, ~2100 sections) must complete — including section summaries — in under 15 minutes on commodity hardware. This is achieved through bounded parallelism, not sequential LLM calls.
Boring technology wins (Core Belief #12): intent classification uses keyword heuristics first, LLM only as fallback. The heuristic is a pure function with a unit test. Reliability over cleverness.

Pure functions for core logic (Core Belief #19): context packing, RRF scoring, intent heuristics are pure functions with no I/O. They live in app/services/ and are tested with plain assert statements.
Every new service method and every new API endpoint must have at least one pytest test. No exceptions.

architecture.layerRules: All new code obeys the existing 6-layer rule: Types → Config → Repo → Service → Runtime → API. SectionSummarizerService is a Service. ChatState TypedDict is a Type. chat_graph.py is a Runtime. No cross-layer imports.

performanceBudget.ingestion_iliad: Full ingestion (parse + chunk + embed + NER + section summaries) <= 15 minutes for a ~180k-word TXT book with ~2100 sections. Section summarization caps at 100 summary units. With Semaphore(10) concurrency and ~3 sec/call: 10 batches * 3 sec = 30 sec for section summaries. Document summary adds ~30 sec. Total background work well under 5 minutes after stage=complete.
performanceBudget.chat_response: First token latency <= 3 seconds for summary-intent queries (pure DB lookup, no retrieval). <= 8 seconds for factual queries (hybrid retrieval + LLM). These are not CI-enforced but Ralph must not introduce regressions observable by a human using the app.
performanceBudget.intent_classification: Keyword heuristic completes in microseconds (no LLM call). LLM fallback for ambiguous cases adds <= 2 seconds.

stories.title: SectionSummaryModel + parallel section summarization node in ingestion pipeline
stories.description: CONTEXT: The ingestion pipeline currently generates document-level summaries only (via background task), using map-reduce over all raw chunks. For a large book like the Iliad (3307 chunks), this requires 100+ sequential LLM calls and either times out or takes 15+ minutes. V2 fixes this by generating section-level summaries during ingestion in parallel, bounded to a maximum of 100 summary units per document regardless of section count. These section summaries become the input to document-level summarization (S76), replacing the slow chunk-based map-reduce. DATA MODEL — Add SectionSummaryModel to app/models.py: class SectionSummaryModel(Base): __tablename__ = 'section_summaries'. Columns: id: Mapped[str] (UUID primary key), document_id: Mapped[str] (non-nullable, indexed), section_id: Mapped[str | None] (nullable — null when the unit spans multiple merged sections), heading: Mapped[str] (the heading or merged headings for this unit, max 200 chars), content: Mapped[str] (the generated 1-2 sentence summary), unit_index: Mapped[int] (0-based position within the document), created_at: Mapped[datetime]. Add this model to app/db_init.py so the table is created on startup alongside other tables. DB MIGRATION: add 'section_summaries' to the tables created in db_init.py. SERVICE — Create app/services/section_summarizer.py. Class SectionSummarizerService. Method: async def generate(self, document_id: str, concurrency: int = 10) -> int. Implementation: (1) Fetch all SectionModel rows for document_id ordered by section_order. (2) Filter: keep only sections where len(section.preview) >= 200 characters. Short headings and near-empty sections produce useless summaries. (3) GROUPING for large documents: if the number of qualifying sections exceeds 100, merge adjacent sections into groups so the total units <= 100. Group by consecutive blocks: group_size = ceil(qualifying_count / 100). Each group's heading = first section heading in the group; each group's text = concatenation of section previews within the group. (4) For each unit (single section or merged group): call LiteLLM with model from get_settings().default_model, system='Summarize the following passage in 1 to 2 sentences. Be specific about names, events, and arguments. Output only the summary.', user=unit_text[:4000] (hard cap to stay within context). (5) Concurrency: use asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency) — NOT asyncio.gather without a semaphore. Wrap each LiteLLM call in the semaphore. This guarantees at most `concurrency` parallel calls at any moment. (6) Store each result as a SectionSummaryModel row as it completes (don't wait for all). (7) Return total count of rows inserted. Error handling: if an individual unit's LLM call fails, log a warning and skip that unit (non-fatal). If Ollama is unreachable (litellm.ServiceUnavailableError), log a warning and return 0 immediately — the document is still usable without section summaries. INGESTION GRAPH — In app/workflows/ingestion.py, add a new node function section_summarize_node(state: IngestionState) -> IngestionState AFTER entity_extract_node and BEFORE summarize_node. The node: imports SectionSummarizerService lazily (same pattern as other nodes), calls await svc.generate(state['document_id']), stores the count in state['section_summary_count'] (add this field to IngestionState TypedDict). The existing summarize_node (which fires background pregenerate) remains unchanged — S76 will modify pregenerate to use section summaries. Wire the new node into the graph: the edge entity_extract_node -> section_summarize_node -> summarize_node. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENT: For the Iliad fixture (~2100 sections grouped to 100 units, Semaphore(10)): section_summarize_node must complete in under 5 minutes with a running Ollama instance. With Semaphore(10) and ~3 sec/call: 100 units / 10 concurrent = 10 sequential batches * ~3 sec = ~30 seconds. The 5-minute budget is conservative. API — Add GET /summarize/{document_id}/sections to app/routers/summarize.py: returns list of {unit_index, heading, content} ordered by unit_index for the given document. Returns [] if no section summaries exist (not an error — document may be pre-v2). TESTS — tests/test_section_summarizer.py: (a) test_grouping_caps_at_100_units: create a document with 300 qualifying sections, call generate() with a mock LiteLLM that returns instantly, assert SectionSummaryModel count <= 100. (b) test_short_sections_skipped: create sections where half have len(preview) < 200, assert only the qualifying half are summarized. (c) test_concurrency_respects_semaphore: mock LiteLLM call with asyncio.sleep(0.01), measure time for 50 units with concurrency=5, assert fewer concurrent calls than total units. (d) test_ollama_offline_returns_zero: mock litellm.acompletion to raise litellm.ServiceUnavailableError, assert generate() returns 0 and does not raise. (e) test_get_sections_endpoint_returns_list: after generate(), GET /summarize/{id}/sections returns list with correct unit_index ordering. SMOKE — scripts/smoke/S75.sh: (1) POST /ingest with a small real TXT fixture, wait for stage=complete by polling GET /documents/{id} every 5s up to 120s. (2) GET /summarize/{id}/sections, assert HTTP 200 and response is a JSON array (may be empty for very small docs). (3) Exit 0.

stories.title: ChatState TypedDict + LangGraph chat router skeleton with intent classifier
stories.description: CONTEXT: The current qa.py stream_answer() is a linear async function that always does: rewrite query → hybrid retrieval → (maybe prepend summary) → LLM call. There is no intelligence about what kind of question is being asked. V2 replaces this with a LangGraph StateGraph in app/runtime/chat_graph.py. This story creates the graph skeleton, the ChatState type, and the intent classifier node. Subsequent stories (S78, S79) fill in the strategy nodes and context packer. TYPES — Create app/types/chat.py. Define: (1) IntentType = Literal['summary', 'factual', 'relational', 'comparative', 'exploratory']. (2) ChatState as a TypedDict with fields: question: str, doc_ids: list[str], scope: Literal['single', 'all'], intent: IntentType | None, rewritten_question: str | None, chunks: list[dict], section_context: str | None, answer: str, citations: list[dict], confidence: Literal['high', 'medium', 'low'], not_found: bool, model: str | None. All fields must have defaults compatible with TypedDict (use total=False for optional fields OR provide all defaults). INTENT CLASSIFIER — The intent classifier runs as the first node in the graph. It must be fast because it adds latency to every query. Strategy: keyword heuristics first (pure function, microseconds), LLM fallback only when heuristics return low confidence. Pure function classify_intent_heuristic(question: str) -> tuple[IntentType, float]: implements the following rules in order (first match wins): (1) If question contains any of [summarize, overview, what is this about, what are the themes, main points, key ideas, synopsis, brief me, give me a summary]: return ('summary', 0.9). (2) If question contains any of [how are, how does, relation between, connection between, what is the relationship, how do X and Y]: return ('relational', 0.85). (3) If question contains any of [compare, difference between, how is X different, versus, vs., similarities]: return ('comparative', 0.85). (4) If question contains any of [explain, what does, who is, where is, when did, which, list all, how many, what happened]: return ('factual', 0.8). (5) Otherwise: return ('exploratory', 0.5). All string matching is case-insensitive. This function lives in app/services/intent.py as a pure function with no imports from other app layers. LLM FALLBACK: if confidence < 0.7, call LiteLLM with system='Classify the question. Reply with exactly one word: summary, factual, relational, comparative, or exploratory.', user=question. Parse response: strip whitespace and lowercase. If response matches one of the five values, use it with confidence=0.8. Otherwise fall back to 'factual' with confidence=0.6. The LLM fallback must be wrapped in try/except — if it fails (Ollama offline), default to 'factual'. GRAPH SKELETON — Create app/runtime/chat_graph.py. Build a LangGraph StateGraph[ChatState] with the following nodes: (1) classify_node: calls classify_intent_heuristic(), if confidence < 0.7 calls LLM fallback, writes intent and (if rewriting enabled) calls existing Kuzu rewriting logic (import from qa.py, do not duplicate). (2) route_node: a conditional edge function, not a node — reads intent from state and routes: 'summary' -> 'summary_node', 'relational' -> 'graph_node', 'comparative' -> 'comparative_node', 'factual' OR 'exploratory' -> 'search_node'. (3) summary_node: STUB for S78 — for now, writes answer='[summary_node stub]' to state, sets confidence='high'. (4) search_node: STUB for S78 — calls the existing _hybrid_retrieval logic from qa.py, writes chunks to state, writes answer='[search_node stub]'. (5) graph_node: STUB for S78 — writes answer='[graph_node stub]'. (6) comparative_node: STUB for S78 — writes answer='[comparative_node stub]'. (7) synthesize_node: STUB for S79 — takes chunks from state, calls LLM, writes answer. For now copies existing LLM call logic from stream_answer(). The graph's entry point is classify_node. All terminal nodes (after their answer is set) route to synthesize_node, then END. QA.PY INTEGRATION — Modify stream_answer() in qa.py to invoke the LangGraph graph instead of its existing logic. The signature of stream_answer() does NOT change — the frontend contract is unchanged. stream_answer() creates the initial ChatState from its parameters, invokes the graph with graph.invoke(state), then streams the answer field token by token using the same SSE format as before. The graph must be compiled once at module load (graph = build_chat_graph().compile()) and reused. IMPORTANT: The streaming behavior from the frontend's perspective must be identical to v1 — the same SSE events with the same fields (token, done, citations, confidence, not_found). The graph's synthesize_node is responsible for calling LLM and accumulating tokens. TESTS — tests/test_intent_classifier.py: (a) test_summary_keywords_detected: each keyword in the summary list triggers intent='summary'. (b) test_comparative_keywords_detected. (c) test_relational_keywords_detected. (d) test_low_confidence_triggers_llm_fallback: mock LiteLLM to return 'factual', assert intent='factual'. (e) test_llm_fallback_offline_defaults_to_factual: mock LiteLLM to raise exception, assert intent='factual'. tests/test_chat_graph.py: (a) test_summary_question_routes_to_summary_node: run graph with question='summarize this book', assert stub answer contains 'summary_node'. (b) test_factual_question_routes_to_search_node. (c) test_graph_invoke_does_not_raise: run full graph with real state dict, assert no exception. SMOKE — scripts/smoke/S77.sh: POST /qa with body {question: 'What are the main themes?', document_ids: [], scope: 'all'}, assert HTTP 200 and response body contains 'answer' field with non-empty string. Exit 0.

stories.title: V2 integration tests, performance benchmarks, and QUALITY_SCORE update
stories.description: CONTEXT: Stories S75-S79 implement the v2 architecture. This story adds integration tests that validate the full v2 pipeline end-to-end on real documents, enforces the performance budget, and updates the quality documentation. Without enforced CI gates, the performance and routing improvements are invisible to future ralph iterations. INTEGRATION TESTS — tests/test_v2_pipeline.py. This test file uses the same session-scoped fixture pattern as test_e2e_book.py (reads from conftest_books.py). Tests assume the three corpus books are already ingested. (a) test_section_summaries_generated_for_all_books: for each of the three books, assert len(SectionSummaryModel rows where document_id=doc.id) >= 10. This verifies section_summarize_node ran successfully. (b) test_document_summary_uses_fast_path: for each book with section summaries, fetch SummaryModel where mode='_section_reduce', assert it exists. This proves pregenerate() used the fast path. (c) test_intent_routing_summary: POST /qa with body {question: 'Give me an overview of this book', document_ids: [alice_id], scope: 'single'}. Assert HTTP 200. Assert response.answer is non-empty. Assert response.confidence in ('high', 'medium'). (d) test_intent_routing_factual: POST /qa with body {question: 'Who is the White Rabbit?', document_ids: [alice_id], scope: 'single'}. Assert HTTP 200 and answer non-empty and at least one citation returned. (e) test_intent_routing_comparative: POST /qa with body {question: 'Compare Alice and Arjuna', document_ids: [], scope: 'all'}. Assert HTTP 200 and answer non-empty. (f) test_intent_routing_relational: POST /qa with body {question: 'How are Achilles and Hector related?', document_ids: [iliad_id], scope: 'single'}. Assert HTTP 200 and answer non-empty. (g) test_library_overview_includes_all_three_books: POST /summarize/all with mode=executive and force_refresh=true. Assert HTTP 200 and streaming response contains content referencing at least two of the three book titles. PERFORMANCE TEST — tests/test_v2_ingestion_perf.py. This test is slow and skipped in CI unless LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS=1 env var is set (use pytest.mark.skipif). When enabled: (a) test_section_summarize_node_under_5_minutes: reset section_summaries for the Iliad, run SectionSummarizerService.generate() on the Iliad document, time it, assert completion in under 300 seconds. (b) test_pregenerate_fast_path_under_3_minutes: ensure section summaries exist, delete existing SummaryModel rows for Iliad, call pregenerate() on Iliad, time it, assert completion in under 180 seconds. MAKEFILE — Add target 'test-v2': runs 'cd backend && uv run pytest tests/test_v2_pipeline.py -v'. Add to Makefile alongside existing 'test-books-all'. The perf tests are NOT part of 'make test-v2' — they require LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS=1. QUALITY SCORE UPDATE — After all tests pass, invoke the luminary-docs agent to update docs/QUALITY_SCORE.md. Changes required: Q&A row: grade B -> A, notes 'v2 agentic router: intent classification, 4 strategy nodes, section-aware context packing'. Ingestion row: notes 'v2: section-level summarization, fast-path document summary, sub-5min for Iliad'. Add a new 'Last Updated' line for v2. TECH DEBT — Add TD-011 to tech-debt-tracker.md: 'pack_context() uses a word-count token approximation (word_count * 1.3). Replace with tiktoken or litellm.token_counter for exact counts, especially if context windows larger than 4k are used.' Priority: low. SMOKE — scripts/smoke/S80.sh: sequence of 4 POST /qa calls with different intent types (summary, factual, relational, comparative), assert all return HTTP 200 and non-empty answer. Assert GET /summarize/{iliad_id}/sections returns non-empty array. Exit 0.

stories.title: Confidence-adaptive retry: augment context with complementary strategies when confidence is low
stories.description:
CONTEXT: The current chat graph always ends after one synthesize call. If the LLM returns confidence='low' (meaning it lacked sufficient context), the answer is returned as-is. This story adds a confidence check gate after synthesize_node that triggers a second retrieval pass using a complementary strategy, then re-synthesizes with the combined richer context.

CURRENT GRAPH TOPOLOGY:
  classify_node → route_node → [summary|graph|comparative|search]_node → synthesize_node → END

NEW TOPOLOGY AFTER THIS STORY:
  classify_node → route_node → [strategy]_node → synthesize_node
    → confidence_gate_node
      → (high|medium confidence, OR retry_attempted=True) → END
      → (low confidence, retry_attempted=False) → augment_node
        → synthesize_node (re-runs with appended context)
        → confidence_gate_node → END (always, retry_attempted=True)

COMPLEMENTARY STRATEGY MAPPING (augment_node selects based on primary_strategy):
  'search' or 'factual' or 'exploratory' → add Kuzu graph entity relationships
    (run _query_kuzu_for_entity for entities extracted from the question)
  'relational' → add hybrid search results (k=15, broader)
  'summary' → add hybrid search results (k=10)
  'comparative' → add hybrid search results with no doc_id filter (k=10)

CONTEXT MERGING: augment_node APPENDS to state['chunks'] and state['section_context'] (does not replace). pack_context() in synthesize_node handles deduplication and token budget enforcement automatically — no special merge logic needed.

LOOP PREVENTION: ChatState gains retry_attempted: bool (default False). route_node sets primary_strategy in state. augment_node sets retry_attempted=True. confidence_gate_node checks retry_attempted before re-routing — guarantees at most 1 retry.

GRAPH KNOWLEDGE WHEREVER NECESSARY: This story ensures graph entity context supplements every factual/exploratory/search answer when the LLM signals insufficient context, not just when the user explicitly asks a relational question.

stories.title: Bug: Key Points tab shows per-passage summaries instead of thematic key points
stories.description: CONTEXT: The 'Key Points' tab in the document reader (mode='executive') calls GET /summarize/{doc_id} with mode=executive. Since S76, the fast path feeds the concatenated section summaries from SectionSummaryModel as input to the executive LLM call. Each section summary is a literal 1-2 sentence description of that passage. When the LLM receives 50-100 such mini-summaries as input, it lists them as individual bullet points (e.g. '1. Holmes investigates a missing coronet... 2. Miss Hunter seeks help... 3. Project Gutenberg is a non-profit...') rather than synthesising overarching thematic key points. Additionally, metadata/licensing sections (Project Gutenberg terms, copyright notices, disclaimers) are included in section_summaries even though the summarisation prompt already says to ignore them -- this is because the section summary for those sections explicitly mentions Gutenberg, so when it becomes the LLM input the instruction is ineffective. ROOT CAUSE: Two layers of failure: (1) SectionSummarizerService.generate() does not filter out metadata/legal sections before generating per-section summaries. (2) The executive mode prompt does not clearly instruct the LLM to synthesise across summaries rather than list them. FIX LAYER 1 -- Metadata section filter in SectionSummarizerService: Add a pure function _is_metadata_section(heading: str, text: str) -> bool to app/services/section_summarizer.py. It returns True if the heading or the first 500 chars of text contain ANY of these case-insensitive signals: ['project gutenberg', 'terms of use', 'license', 'disclaimer', 'copyright', 'trademark', 'legal', 'distribution', 'reproduction', 'permitted use', 'electronic work', 'archive foundation']. In generate(), skip any qualifying section where _is_metadata_section(section.heading, section.preview) is True, BEFORE the preview length check. Add a log line at DEBUG level: 'Skipping metadata section: %s', heading. FIX LAYER 2 -- Metadata filter in _build_section_summary_input(): In SummarizationService._build_section_summary_input(), after fetching all SectionSummaryModel rows, filter out any row where _is_metadata_section(row.heading, row.content) is True. Import _is_metadata_section from app/services/section_summarizer. If the filtered list has fewer than 3 rows, return None (fall through to slow path -- avoids generating an executive summary from metadata only). FIX LAYER 3 -- Executive prompt improvement: In summarizer.py MODE_INSTRUCTIONS['executive'], replace the existing instruction with: 'Identify the 3 to 5 most important overarching themes, arguments, or narrative ideas that run through the entire work. Write each as a concise bullet point. Do NOT list individual chapter or passage summaries -- synthesise across them. Focus on ideas, arguments, character arcs, and findings. Ignore copyright notices, licensing terms, and distribution metadata. ' + _MARKDOWN_INSTRUCTION. IMPORTANT: The _is_metadata_section function must be importable from section_summarizer without circular imports. It is a pure function at module level with no imports from other app layers. TESTS -- tests/test_summary_quality.py: (a) test_metadata_section_filtered_in_generate: create sections including one with heading='Terms of Use' and text containing 'Project Gutenberg'; call generate() with mock LiteLLM; assert mock was NOT called for the metadata section. (b) test_metadata_section_filtered_in_build_input: insert SectionSummaryModel rows including one with heading='License' and content='Project Gutenberg terms...'; call _build_section_summary_input(); assert the returned string does NOT contain 'Project Gutenberg'. (c) test_build_input_returns_none_when_only_metadata: insert 2 non-metadata rows and 3 metadata rows; assert return value is None (fewer than 3 qualifying rows). (d) test_executive_prompt_not_listing_summaries: mock LiteLLM to capture the system prompt sent; call pregenerate() with mock section summaries; assert the system prompt contains 'synthesise' or 'overarching' (case-insensitive). (e) test_is_metadata_section_true_for_gutenberg: assert _is_metadata_section('Project Gutenberg License', 'This eBook is for use of anyone...') returns True. (f) test_is_metadata_section_false_for_content: assert _is_metadata_section('Chapter 1', 'Holmes examined the room carefully...') returns False. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S82.sh: (1) POST /summarize/{sherlock_doc_id} with mode=executive and force_refresh=true, stream response. (2) Assert HTTP 200. (3) Assert the streaming output does NOT contain 'Project Gutenberg' or 'distribution'. (4) Exit 0.

stories.title: Bug: Low confidence on multi-doc scope queries and missing library summary fallback
stories.description: CONTEXT: Two distinct low-confidence scenarios reported: (1) Multi-doc scope (scope='all') factual queries retrieve chunks from all documents, producing a scattered context the LLM cannot synthesise well, leading to confidence='low'. (2) Summary intent with scope='all' when no LibrarySummaryModel exists: summary_node currently falls through to search_node, which performs a library-wide retrieval -- the LLM receives a huge context window of mixed content and returns low confidence. DIAGNOSIS: In app/runtime/chat_graph.py, the summary_node for scope='all' queries LibrarySummaryModel. If no row exists, it overrides intent to 'factual' and returns, re-routing to search_node. search_node runs hybrid retrieval across all documents (no doc_id filter), retrieving up to k=10 chunks from potentially dozens of documents. The synthesize_node system prompt for 'factual' intent does not tell the LLM it is answering a library-wide question. Additionally, _split_response() in qa.py defaults confidence to 'low' when no JSON block is found in the LLM response -- any response that does not append the citation JSON block is penalised. FIX 1 -- Library summary missing: In summary_node, when scope='all' and no LibrarySummaryModel exists: instead of falling through to search, fire a background task to call POST /summarize/all with mode=executive (using fire_and_forget from the patterns in main.py), then write state['answer'] = 'The library summary is being generated. Please ask again in a moment.', state['confidence'] = 'medium', state['not_found'] = False. Return this state directly -- synthesize_node skips the LLM call when answer is already set (existing pass-through logic from S78 handles this). FIX 2 -- Multi-doc factual context: In search_node, when scope='all', reduce k from 10 to 6 and add a diversify step: ensure at most 2 chunks per document_id in the result set. Implement _cap_per_document(chunks: list[dict], max_per_doc: int = 2) -> list[dict] as a pure function in context_packer.py. FIX 3 -- Confidence default: In qa.py _split_response(), when no JSON block is present but the answer text is non-empty (len(answer) > 80 chars), default confidence to 'medium' (not 'low'). Only return 'low' when answer is empty or len(answer) <= 80 chars. FIX 4 -- Synthesize prompt for multi-doc: In synthesize_node, when scope == 'all' and intent in ('factual', 'exploratory'), append to the system prompt: 'The user is asking about their entire library. Answer using only the provided passages. If the passages come from multiple documents, synthesise across them. Be explicit about which document each point comes from.' TESTS -- tests/test_confidence_fixes.py: (a) test_library_summary_missing_returns_medium: mock LibrarySummaryModel query to return None; run summary_node with scope='all'; assert state['confidence'] == 'medium' and 'being generated' in state['answer']. (b) test_split_response_defaults_medium_for_long_answer: call _split_response with a long answer string (>80 chars) containing no JSON block; assert confidence == 'medium'. (c) test_split_response_defaults_low_for_short_answer: call _split_response('I do not know.'); assert confidence == 'low'. (d) test_cap_per_document_limits_chunks: call _cap_per_document with 6 chunks from 2 documents (3 each), max_per_doc=2; assert len(result) == 4 and each doc_id appears at most twice. (e) test_search_node_caps_multi_doc_chunks: run search_node with scope='all' and mock retrieval returning 5 chunks from the same doc_id; assert output chunks contain at most 2 from that document. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S83.sh: (1) POST /qa with scope='all', question='Who is the main character?'; assert HTTP 200, done event confidence != 'low'. (2) POST /qa with scope='all', question='Summarize all documents' when no library summary cached; assert HTTP 200, done event answer is non-empty. Exit 0.

stories.title: Bug: UI responsiveness -- lazy-load core pages, fix Notes stale time, remove duplicate SSE calls
stories.description: CONTEXT: Navigation between tabs feels sluggish and some UI elements take visible time to load. Root causes identified by reading App.tsx and Notes.tsx: (1) Chat, Learning, Notes, Study are imported eagerly in App.tsx (lines 18-21) -- only Viz and Monitoring use React.lazy. Chat and Learning have heavy dependency trees (SSE streaming, document reader, library components). (2) Notes.tsx overrides the global staleTime (60_000) with staleTime: 10_000 on both its useQuery calls, causing a refetch on almost every tab switch. (3) LibraryOverview in Learning.tsx calls generate() on mount with no guard -- in React StrictMode (development), double-mount fires two parallel SSE streams for the same mode. (4) Notes list view TableRow has a dead onClick stub comment that creates a no-op event handler on every row render. FIX 1 -- Lazy-load Chat, Learning, Notes, Study in App.tsx: Replace the four direct imports with React.lazy + Suspense. Use the exact pattern already applied to Viz and Monitoring. Each page uses the existing PageSkeleton fallback. The four replacements: const Chat = lazy(() => import('./pages/Chat')), const Learning = lazy(() => import('./pages/Learning')), const Notes = lazy(() => import('./pages/Notes')), const Study = lazy(() => import('./pages/Study')). Wrap each Route element in <Suspense fallback={<PageSkeleton />}>. Add a comment in App.tsx listing all lazy-loaded pages. FIX 2 -- Notes stale time: In Notes.tsx, remove the explicit staleTime: 10_000 override from the notes list useQuery and the notes-groups useQuery. Let them inherit the global 60_000 default from QueryClient defaultOptions. FIX 3 -- LibraryOverview mount guard: In Learning.tsx LibraryOverview component, add const generatedModes = useRef(new Set<string>()). In the generate() function, at the top: if (generatedModes.current.has(mode) && !forceRefresh) return. After starting the stream: generatedModes.current.add(mode). This prevents duplicate SSE calls on React StrictMode double-mount. The forceRefresh parameter bypasses the guard so manual refresh still works. FIX 4 -- Notes list view onClick stub: In Notes.tsx, the TableRow onClick is a dead comment stub. Replace it with onClick={() => setEditingNote(note)} where editingNote is new state const [editingNote, setEditingNote] = useState<Note | null>(null). When editingNote is set, render a NoteEditorTabs component below the table in a bordered card with a close button. This is a minimal fix to remove the dead stub -- the full focused dialog is S87. TESTS -- No new pytest tests required (UI-only changes). TypeScript must pass: npx tsc --noEmit exits 0. npm run build from frontend/ exits 0. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S84.sh: (1) npm run build from frontend/ -- exit 0 required. (2) npx tsc --noEmit from frontend/ -- exit 0 required. Exit 0.

stories.title: Feature: RAGAS eval results panel in Monitoring tab with backend trigger endpoint
stories.description: CONTEXT: RAGAS evaluation already exists as a standalone script at evals/run_eval.py. It reads golden datasets from evals/golden/, runs RAGAS metrics (answer_relevancy, context_precision, context_recall, faithfulness), and appends scores to evals/scores_history.jsonl. This story integrates these results into the Monitoring tab so users can see eval quality at a glance and trigger re-runs from the UI. The existing evals/ setup must not be modified -- the backend reads from the same files the CLI script writes to. BACKEND -- Add two endpoints to a new router app/routers/evals.py. Register the router in app/main.py with prefix='/evals'. Endpoint 1: GET /evals/results. Reads REPO_ROOT/evals/scores_history.jsonl where REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent (four levels up from app/routers/evals.py reaches the monorepo root). Returns the most recent result per dataset as a JSON array: [{dataset: str, run_at: str (ISO), hit_rate_5: float | null, mrr: float | null, faithfulness: float | null, context_precision: float | null, context_recall: float | null, answer_relevancy: float | null, passed_thresholds: bool | null}]. If the file does not exist, return []. Parse each line as JSON and skip malformed lines. Group by dataset, take the row with the latest run_at. Endpoint 2: POST /evals/run. Accepts Pydantic body: EvalRunRequest with fields dataset: Literal['book', 'paper', 'conversation', 'notes', 'code'] and assert_thresholds: bool = False. Returns HTTP 202 immediately with {status: 'started', dataset: dataset}. Fires a background task using fire_and_forget that calls asyncio.to_thread(subprocess.run, cmd, cwd=..., capture_output=True) where cmd = ['uv', 'run', 'python', 'run_eval.py', '--dataset', dataset] optionally appending ['--assert-thresholds']. On completion, log stdout/stderr at DEBUG level. IMPORTANT: asyncio.to_thread is required -- subprocess.run blocks the event loop; never use it directly in an async function without to_thread. FRONTEND -- In Monitoring.tsx, add an 'Eval Results' section. Component EvalPanel: (1) useQuery(['eval-results'], fetchEvalResults, {staleTime: 120_000}) where fetchEvalResults calls GET /evals/results. (2) Render a table: columns Dataset | Hit Rate @5 | MRR | Faithfulness | Context Precision | Last Run | Actions. Each numeric score rendered with a coloured indicator: >= threshold shows a green check icon + value, < threshold shows an amber warning icon + value, null shows a grey dash. Thresholds: hit_rate_5 >= 0.60, mrr >= 0.45, faithfulness >= 0.65 (match evals/run_eval.py THRESHOLDS). (3) 'Run Eval' button per row calls POST /evals/run {dataset: row.dataset}. Shows Loader2 spinner when mutation isPending. On success, show a sonner toast 'Evaluation started for {dataset}'. After 5 seconds, invalidate ['eval-results'] query via setTimeout + queryClient.invalidateQueries. (4) Loading state: 3 Skeleton rows. Error state: amber alert 'Could not load eval results'. Empty state: 'No evaluations run yet. Click Run Eval to start.' TESTS -- tests/test_evals_router.py: (a) test_get_results_empty_when_no_file: GET /evals/results when scores_history.jsonl does not exist returns HTTP 200 and []. (b) test_get_results_returns_latest_per_dataset: write a scores_history.jsonl with 2 book entries (different run_at) and 1 paper entry to a tmp dir; mock REPO_ROOT; GET /evals/results returns 2 items (one per dataset, most recent book entry selected). (c) test_post_run_returns_202: POST /evals/run {dataset: 'book'} returns HTTP 202 and body {status: 'started'}. (d) test_post_run_invalid_dataset: POST /evals/run {dataset: 'invalid'} returns HTTP 422. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S85.sh: (1) GET /evals/results returns HTTP 200 and a JSON array. (2) POST /evals/run {dataset: 'book'} returns HTTP 202. Exit 0.

stories.title: Feature: Entity disambiguation -- canonical name normalisation before Kuzu upsert
stories.description: CONTEXT: The knowledge graph contains duplicate nodes for the same real-world entity because GLiNER extracts surface-form variants: 'Holmes', 'Sherlock Holmes', 'Mr. Holmes' become three separate PERSON nodes. These duplicates fragment the graph, lower retrieval relevance, and make entity-based navigation confusing. The disambiguation pass handles three cases: honorific prefixes (Mr., Mrs., Dr., Sir, Miss, Lady, Lord, Prof.), substring containment (shorter name contained in longer), and token overlap (2+ shared tokens). NOTE: 'Sr. Holmes' is intentionally distinct from 'Sherlock Holmes' -- 'Sr.' is not an honorific in _HONORIFICS; only substring containment or token overlap would merge it. NEW FILE -- Create app/services/entity_disambiguator.py. HONORIFIC STRIPPING: Define _HONORIFICS = frozenset({'mr', 'mrs', 'ms', 'dr', 'sir', 'miss', 'lady', 'lord', 'prof', 'professor', 'rev', 'captain', 'capt', 'col', 'gen', 'sgt', 'lt'}). Pure function _strip_honorifics(name: str) -> str: lowercase the name, split on whitespace, drop any leading token that is in _HONORIFICS after stripping trailing punctuation. Return remaining tokens joined by space. CANONICAL SELECTION: Method find_canonical(name: str, entity_type: str, existing_names: list[str]) -> str: (1) Compute stripped = _strip_honorifics(name). (2) For each existing in existing_names: compute stripped_existing = _strip_honorifics(existing). (3) RULE A -- Exact stripped match (case-insensitive): return existing. (4) RULE B -- Substring containment: if stripped is a substring of stripped_existing OR vice versa, AND (both have >= 2 tokens OR the shorter has >= 5 chars): return the longer of (name, existing). (5) RULE C -- Token overlap: if the sets of lowercase tokens share >= 2 tokens: return the longer of (name, existing). (6) If no match: return name as its own canonical. Method canonicalize_batch(entities: list[dict], entity_type: str) -> list[dict]: process entities of the given type in order. Maintain running dict canonical_map keyed by _strip_honorifics(canonical_name). For each entity, call find_canonical against canonical_map.values(); update canonical_map; update entity['name'] to resolved canonical. Return updated list. INTEGRATION -- In app/services/graph.py, in the method that writes entities to Kuzu (find the upsert/MERGE loop), after receiving the extracted entity list and before any Kuzu write, instantiate EntityDisambiguator and call canonicalize_batch for each of PERSON, ORGANIZATION, PLACE. The Kuzu MERGE must use the canonical name. ALIASES -- When a non-canonical name is merged to a canonical, append the non-canonical name to a node aliases property stored as a comma-separated string (since Kuzu embedded may not support array properties natively -- check existing Kuzu schema and follow the same pattern used for other multi-value fields). Add the aliases property to the entity node schema in db_init.py or wherever the Kuzu schema is initialised. TESTS -- tests/test_entity_disambiguator.py: (a) test_honorific_stripped_resolves_to_existing: find_canonical('Mr. Holmes', 'PERSON', ['Sherlock Holmes']) -- stripped 'holmes' is substring of 'sherlock holmes' (Rule B) -- returns 'Sherlock Holmes'. (b) test_direct_substring: find_canonical('Holmes', 'PERSON', ['Sherlock Holmes']) returns 'Sherlock Holmes'. (c) test_longer_name_wins: find_canonical('Sherlock Holmes', 'PERSON', ['Holmes']) returns 'Sherlock Holmes'. (d) test_no_match_different_person: find_canonical('Watson', 'PERSON', ['Holmes']) returns 'Watson'. (e) test_sr_not_in_honorifics: assert 'sr' not in _HONORIFICS; find_canonical('Sr. Holmes', 'PERSON', ['Sherlock Holmes', 'Holmes']) -- 'sr. holmes' is not a substring of 'sherlock holmes'; token overlap {'sr.', 'holmes'} vs {'sherlock', 'holmes'} = 1 shared token (less than 2) -- returns 'Sr. Holmes' (distinct). (f) test_canonicalize_batch_deduplicates_variants: input [{'name': 'Holmes', 'entity_type': 'PERSON'}, {'name': 'Mr. Holmes', 'entity_type': 'PERSON'}, {'name': 'Sherlock Holmes', 'entity_type': 'PERSON'}]; assert all three resolve to 'Sherlock Holmes'. (g) test_entity_type_boundary: PLACE 'London' does not merge with PERSON 'Jack London' -- entity_type filter prevents cross-type matches. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S86.sh: (1) Ingest a small fixture doc containing 'Holmes', 'Mr. Holmes', 'Sherlock Holmes'. (2) GET /graph/entities?document_id={id}. (3) Assert response JSON does not contain both 'Holmes' and 'Sherlock Holmes' as separate PERSON entries. Exit 0.

stories.title: Feature: Notes focused editor dialog with side-by-side Write/Preview for list and card views
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Notes page has two views: card (NoteCard grid with inline edit) and list (Table). Problems: (1) List view TableRow has a dead onClick stub -- clicking a row does nothing. (2) NoteCard inline edit collapses the card into a textarea, hiding all surrounding context. (3) Neither view offers simultaneous Write + Preview. The user wants a focused dialog where typing in the Write pane instantly updates the Preview pane side-by-side. NEW COMPONENT -- Create frontend/src/components/NoteEditorDialog.tsx. Use shadcn/ui Dialog (Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogFooter from '@/components/ui/dialog'). Props: { note: Note | null, onClose: () => void, onSaved: (updated: Note) => void }. Dialog is open when note is not null. DialogContent: max-w-4xl, h-[70vh], overflow-hidden. Inside: two-column grid (grid-cols-2 gap-0 h-full). Left column header 'Write', right column header 'Preview', separated by a vertical border. Left pane: textarea full-height, monospace font, controlled by local state const [content, setContent] = useState(note?.content ?? ''). The textarea re-initialises from note.content when note prop changes (useEffect with note?.id as dep). Right pane: MarkdownRenderer rendering content in real time. stories.title: Feature: Expand golden datasets to Alice in Wonderland and The Odyssey; corpus-driven integration tests
stories.description: CONTEXT: The RAGAS golden dataset at evals/golden/book.jsonl has 30 entries, all from The Time Machine. Alice in Wonderland and The Odyssey are registered in DATA/books/manifest.json and used in conftest_books.py ingestion fixtures, but have zero golden Q&A coverage. This means RAGAS measures retrieval quality on a single Victorian sci-fi novella -- it will not catch regressions in literary fiction (Alice) or epic poetry/prose translation (Odyssey). This story adds 20 golden entries per missing book and creates a new integration test file that runs retrieval, summarization, and entity extraction assertions against all three corpus books on real ingested data. IMPORTANT: The context_hint field in every golden entry must be a verbatim lowercase substring that genuinely appears in the source text. Ralph must read each source file before writing context_hint values to verify they match. GOLDEN DATASET EXPANSION -- Add exactly 20 new entries to evals/golden/book.jsonl for Alice in Wonderland (source_file: DATA/books/alice_in_wonderland.txt) and 20 new entries for The Odyssey (source_file: DATA/books/the_odyssey.txt). Use the exact same JSONL schema as existing entries: {question, ground_truth_answer, context_hint, document_id, source_file}. Set document_id to 'TBD' -- the RAGAS runner resolves it at runtime. Alice in Wonderland required golden entries (Ralph must verify each context_hint by reading the source file): (1) question='How does Alice enter Wonderland?', ground_truth='She follows a White Rabbit down a rabbit hole', context_hint='rabbit-hole'. (2) question='What does Alice drink to become smaller?', ground_truth='A bottle labelled DRINK ME', context_hint='drink me'. (3) question='What does Alice eat to grow taller?', ground_truth='A small cake labelled EAT ME', context_hint='eat me'. (4) question='Who is the Cheshire Cat?', ground_truth='A cat that can disappear leaving only its grin', context_hint='cheshire cat'. (5) question='What game does the Queen of Hearts play?', ground_truth='Croquet using flamingos as mallets and hedgehogs as balls', context_hint='croquet'. (6) question='Who is the Mad Hatter?', ground_truth='A character who holds a perpetual tea party', context_hint='hatter'. (7) question='Who is accused of stealing the tarts?', ground_truth='The Knave of Hearts', context_hint='knave of hearts'. (8) question='What does the Queen of Hearts frequently shout?', ground_truth='Off with their heads', context_hint='off with their heads'. (9) question='Who paints the roses red?', ground_truth='Playing card gardeners who accidentally planted white roses and try to hide the mistake', context_hint='painting them red'. (10) question='What is the name of Alice cat?', ground_truth='Dinah', context_hint='dinah'. (11) question='What creature does the Duchess carry as a baby?', ground_truth='A baby that turns into a pig', context_hint='turned into a pig'. (12) question='What is the Caterpillar smoking?', ground_truth='A hookah', context_hint='hookah'. (13) question='What does the Mock Turtle teach?', ground_truth='Reading, Writing, and Arithmetic along with Uglification and Derision', context_hint='mock turtle'. (14) question='How does Alice grow too large for the White Rabbit house?', ground_truth='She drinks from a bottle inside the house and grows until she fills the room', context_hint='too large'. (15) question='Who are at the Mad Tea Party?', ground_truth='The Mad Hatter, the March Hare, and the Dormouse', context_hint='march hare'. (16) question='What verdict does the King try to pronounce before the jury deliberates?', ground_truth='Sentence first, verdict afterwards', context_hint='sentence first'. (17) question='Who is the White Rabbit?', ground_truth='A rabbit carrying a watch who leads Alice into Wonderland', context_hint='white rabbit'. (18) question='What dance do the Mock Turtle and Gryphon demonstrate?', ground_truth='The Lobster Quadrille', context_hint='lobster quadrille'. (19) question='What exclamation does Alice make when things grow strange?', ground_truth='Curiouser and curiouser', context_hint='curiouser and curiouser'. (20) question='Who does the Cheshire Cat say is mad in Wonderland?', ground_truth='Everyone, including Alice', context_hint='we are all mad here'. The Odyssey required golden entries (Ralph must verify each context_hint): (1) question='Who is Odysseus?', ground_truth='King of Ithaca, hero of the Trojan War, trying to sail home', context_hint='ithaca'. (2) question='What is the name of the Cyclops Odysseus blinds?', ground_truth='Polyphemus', context_hint='polyphemus'. (3) question='How does Odysseus escape the Cyclops cave?', ground_truth='By hiding under the bellies of sheep as they leave', context_hint='sheep'. (4) question='Who is Penelope?', ground_truth='Odysseus faithful wife who waits in Ithaca', context_hint='penelope'. (5) question='What trick does Penelope use to delay the suitors?', ground_truth='She weaves a shroud by day and secretly unravels it at night', context_hint='weaving'. (6) question='Who is Telemachus?', ground_truth='The son of Odysseus and Penelope', context_hint='telemachus'. (7) question='Which goddess assists Odysseus throughout his journey?', ground_truth='Athena', context_hint='athena'. (8) question='Which god opposes Odysseus return home?', ground_truth='Poseidon, angry because Odysseus blinded his son Polyphemus', context_hint='poseidon'. (9) question='Who is Circe?', ground_truth='A sorceress on the island of Aeaea who turns men into pigs', context_hint='circe'. (10) question='What does Circe do to Odysseus crew?', ground_truth='Turns them into pigs', context_hint='pigs'. (11) question='What danger do the Sirens pose?', ground_truth='Their irresistible song lures sailors to their deaths', context_hint='sirens'. (12) question='How does Odysseus survive the Sirens song?', ground_truth='He has himself tied to the mast while his crew plugs their ears with wax', context_hint='mast'. (13) question='Where does Calypso keep Odysseus?', ground_truth='On the island of Ogygia for seven years', context_hint='ogygia'. (14) question='Who is Calypso?', ground_truth='A nymph who detains Odysseus on her island and offers him immortality', context_hint='calypso'. (15) question='What is the name of the faithful swineherd who shelters Odysseus?', ground_truth='Eumaeus', context_hint='eumaeus'. (16) question='How does Odysseus disguise himself when he returns to Ithaca?', ground_truth='As a beggar', context_hint='beggar'. (17) question='What contest does Penelope set for the suitors?', ground_truth='To string Odysseus bow and shoot an arrow through twelve axe heads', context_hint='bow'. (18) question='What is the name of Odysseus faithful old dog?', ground_truth='Argos', context_hint='argos'. (19) question='Who are the suitors?', ground_truth='Men who court Penelope and consume Odysseus household while he is away', context_hint='suitors'. (20) question='What wooden device did Odysseus devise to defeat Troy?', ground_truth='The Trojan Horse', context_hint='horse'. INTEGRATION TESTS -- Create backend/tests/test_corpus_qa.py. This file uses pytest_plugins = ['tests.conftest_books'] and the all_books_ingested session fixture. All tests are marked @pytest.mark.slow. Tests: (a) test_alice_factual_retrieval_hr3: for each of 5 selected Alice golden questions (rabbit-hole, drink me, cheshire cat, mad hatter, queen of hearts), run GET /search?q={question}&document_id={alice_doc_id} and assert context_hint substring appears in at least one of the top-3 returned chunk texts. Assert overall HR@3 >= 0.6 (3 of 5 must hit). (b) test_odyssey_factual_retrieval_hr3: same pattern for Odyssey (polyphemus, circe, penelope weaving, argos, sirens). Assert HR@3 >= 0.6. (c) test_alice_known_entities_extracted: GET /graph/entities?document_id={alice_doc_id}, assert response contains PERSON entities including 'Alice' and 'Queen' (case-insensitive). Assert at least 10 distinct entity names total. (d) test_odyssey_known_entities_extracted: assert entities include 'Odysseus', 'Penelope', 'Telemachus'. At least 15 distinct entity names. (e) test_alice_section_summaries_no_gutenberg: GET /summarize/{alice_doc_id}/sections, assert HTTP 200, assert no section summary content contains 'project gutenberg' or 'terms of use' (case-insensitive). Assert at least 5 section summaries returned. (f) test_odyssey_section_summaries_no_gutenberg: same for Odyssey. (g) test_alice_executive_summary_quality: GET /summarize/{alice_doc_id}/cached, assert 'executive' mode summary exists and does not contain numbered list patterns like '1.' at the start of 3+ lines (which would indicate per-passage listing). Assert summary mentions at least one of: 'alice', 'wonderland', 'queen', 'rabbit', 'mad' (case-insensitive). (h) test_odyssey_executive_summary_quality: assert summary mentions at least one of: 'odysseus', 'penelope', 'trojan', 'journey', 'hero' (case-insensitive). (i) test_cross_book_retrieval: GET /search?q=brave hero on a long journey home with no document_id filter; assert results include chunks from at least 2 different document_ids (cross-document retrieval working). (j) test_all_books_have_section_summaries: for each of the 3 books, GET /summarize/{doc_id}/sections and assert at least 10 rows returned (section summaries were generated during ingestion). RAGAS RUNNER UPDATE -- In evals/run_eval.py, update the per-entry document resolution to support multiple source files in the same JSONL. The existing load_manifest()/ensure_ingested() already handles this via the source_file -> document_id map. No change to the runner is needed for multi-book support -- verify this is true by reading the runner code before claiming. If the runner only supports one document per dataset run (e.g. it assumes all entries have the same source_file), fix it to group entries by source_file, resolve each group's document_id separately, and merge results before scoring. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S88.sh: (1) Count entries in evals/golden/book.jsonl, assert >= 70. (2) grep alice_in_wonderland evals/golden/book.jsonl, assert at least 20 matches. (3) grep the_odyssey evals/golden/book.jsonl, assert at least 20 matches. Exit 0.

stories.title: Bug: Notes markdown rendering -- headings and emphasis unstyled, code blocks uncolored
stories.description: CONTEXT: MarkdownRenderer wraps react-markdown with remark-gfm and rehype-highlight, and applies Tailwind prose classes (prose prose-sm dark:prose-invert max-w-none). Markdown headings (# H1, ## H2), bold (**text** or __text__), italic, and blockquotes render as unstyled plain text. Code blocks have token class names but no colors. ROOT CAUSE 1 -- @tailwindcss/typography is absent from frontend/package.json devDependencies and absent from the plugins array in tailwind.config.cjs. Without this plugin, the prose-* classes compile to nothing. Verify: grep -r "tailwindcss/typography" frontend/ returns empty. ROOT CAUSE 2 -- highlight.js CSS theme is not imported. frontend/src/main.tsx imports only "./index.css". frontend/src/index.css has no highlight.js import. rehype-highlight adds CSS class names but no stylesheet provides the colors. Verify: grep -r "highlight.js" frontend/src/ returns empty. FIX STEP 1: from frontend/ run: npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography. FIX STEP 2: in frontend/tailwind.config.cjs change plugins: [] to plugins: [require("@tailwindcss/typography")]. FIX STEP 3: add the following line at the top of frontend/src/index.css: @import "highlight.js/styles/github-dark.css"; -- highlight.js is already in dependencies so no npm install needed. FIX STEP 4: run npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build from frontend/ to confirm no regressions. No backend changes. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S89.sh: (1) cd frontend && npm run build exits 0. (2) grep "@tailwindcss/typography" tailwind.config.cjs asserts match. (3) grep "highlight.js" src/index.css asserts match. Exit 0.

stories.title: Bug+Feature: Notes auto-tagging -- tags editable, suggestions visible, offline graceful
stories.description: CONTEXT: S90 was marked passes=true but user verification found: (1) no tag suggestions ever appeared after saving a note; (2) no progress indicator while suggestions were being fetched; (3) no way to cancel a pending suggestion fetch; (4) existing tags were read-only in the editor -- no way to add or remove a tag from the dialog. ROOT CAUSES identified by reading the implementation: (A) The create note form in Notes.tsx uses a plain tagsRaw text input and calls POST /notes directly -- suggest-tags is never fired after note creation, only after editing in NoteEditorDialog. New notes never receive suggestions. (B) If Ollama is offline, ServiceUnavailableError is caught and [] is returned. The dialog sees novel.length==0 and immediately calls onClose() with no feedback. The user never learns that auto-suggest was attempted and failed. (C) In NoteEditorDialog the tags section renders current note.tags as read-only chips (no X to remove, no input to add). This story fixes all three issues. BACKEND -- No changes needed. The POST /notes/{note_id}/suggest-tags endpoint is correct. FRONTEND CHANGE 1 -- EDITABLE TAGS IN NoteEditorDialog: Replace the read-only tags section (the div below the Write pane) with a TagEditor inline component (no separate file needed, define inside NoteEditorDialog.tsx). TagEditor manages a local list: const [editTags, setEditTags] = useState<string[]>(note?.tags ?? []). Re-initialise editTags when note.id changes (add to the existing useEffect). Display current editTags as chips each with an X button: clicking X removes the tag from editTags (setEditTags(prev => prev.filter(t => t !== tag))). Below the chips add a small inline text input (placeholder 'Add tag...'): on Enter key or comma key, trim the input value, if non-empty and not already in editTags add it to editTags and clear the input. Tags are saved to the note by passing editTags when calling patchNote -- change the save mutation to patchNote(note.id, {content, tags: editTags}). This means save always persists both content and current tags. FRONTEND CHANGE 2 -- AUTO-SUGGEST AFTER SAVE (both create and edit): After a successful save, fire POST /notes/{note_id}/suggest-tags. Show progress explicitly: while isFetchingTags is true, show a small Loader2 spinner with text 'Suggesting tags...' in the tags area (visible even if the dialog is about to show the Done button). Do not close the dialog until either (a) suggestions arrive and user interacts, or (b) user clicks Done/Cancel explicitly. If suggest-tags succeeds and returns novel tags (not already in editTags), add them to a suggestedTags list and display them as dashed-border chips the user can click to accept (clicking moves the tag into editTags and calls patchNote({tags: [...editTags, tag]})). A Dismiss link removes the suggestion row. If suggest-tags returns [] (Ollama offline or no suggestions), show a brief inline message in the tags area: 'No suggestions available' for 2 seconds, then hide it. Do not silently close -- always show the Done button and let the user close explicitly after seeing the save-succeeded state. FRONTEND CHANGE 3 -- AUTO-SUGGEST IN CREATE FORM: In Notes.tsx the create form calls createNote() on success via the createMut mutation. In createMut.onSuccess, after the note is created, fire fetchSuggestedTags(created.id). If tags are returned, call patchNote(created.id, {tags: suggestions}) silently in the background. Then invalidate the notes query. No UI needed for this -- the note will appear in the list with tags already applied. This is a fire-and-forget improvement; if it fails, the note still exists with no tags (acceptable). FRONTEND CHANGE 4 -- ABORT ON CLOSE: Use a ref to store the current suggest-tags AbortController: const abortRef = useRef<AbortController | null>(null). In fetchSuggestedTags, pass the signal. When the dialog closes (onOpenChange fires with open=false, or Cancel is clicked), call abortRef.current?.abort(). This prevents the async setState calls on an unmounted component and avoids React warnings. TESTS -- Update backend/tests/test_note_tagger.py to add: test_suggest_tags_api_returns_empty_not_503: mock get_note_tagger to return [] (Ollama down path); POST /notes/{id}/suggest-tags; assert HTTP 200 and tags=[] (not HTTP 503 -- offline is graceful). This verifies the critical invariant that offline does not break the UX contract. Frontend: npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build must pass. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S90.sh: (1) Create note via POST /notes body='{"content":"Alice follows the White Rabbit"}'; capture note_id. (2) POST /notes/{note_id}/suggest-tags; assert HTTP 200 and response has 'tags' key. (3) POST /notes/nonexistent/suggest-tags; assert HTTP 404. Exit 0.

stories.title: Feature: Notes full-text and semantic search
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes are isolated -- no free-text or semantic search exists. GET /notes supports tag/group filters only. This story adds FTS5 keyword search and LanceDB semantic search for notes, following the exact same patterns used for chunks. BACKEND -- FTS5: In db_init.py add: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes_fts USING fts5(content, note_id UNINDEXED) after the existing chunks_fts DDL. FTS5 SYNC -- In backend/app/routers/notes.py, add raw SQL sync statements after each write operation (same pattern as chunks_fts sync in documents.py using session.execute(text(...))). On note create (after session.commit()): INSERT INTO notes_fts(rowid, content, note_id) VALUES ((SELECT rowid FROM notes WHERE id=:note_id), :content, :note_id). On note update/patch (after session.commit()): DELETE FROM notes_fts WHERE note_id=:note_id; then INSERT as above. On note delete (before or after session.commit()): DELETE FROM notes_fts WHERE note_id=:note_id. Use the existing _sanitize_fts_query helper from app/services/retriever.py (import it) for query sanitization. LANCEDB -- In backend/app/services/vector_store.py, add alongside the existing chunk_vectors table: NOTE_TABLE_NAME = 'note_vectors'. NOTE_SCHEMA = pa.schema([pa.field('note_id', pa.string()), pa.field('content', pa.string()), pa.field('vector', pa.list_(pa.float32(), 1024))]). Add to LanceDBService: _get_or_create_note_table() -> lancedb.Table (same pattern as _get_or_create_table()). upsert_note_vector(note_id: str, content: str, vector: list[float]) -> None: uses merge_insert on note_id key. delete_note_vector(note_id: str) -> None. EMBEDDING TRIGGER -- In notes.py router, after note create and update, trigger note embedding as a background task using the fire_and_forget helper (import from app.runtime.chat_graph or wherever it is defined -- grep for fire_and_forget first). The coroutine to fire: async def _embed_note(note_id, content) that calls get_embedding_service().encode([content])[0] in a thread executor (run_in_executor, same pattern as ingestion.py line 554), then calls get_lancedb_service().upsert_note_vector(note_id, content, vector). On note delete, call get_lancedb_service().delete_note_vector(note_id) synchronously (no background needed, it's fast). SEARCH SERVICE -- Create backend/app/services/note_search.py. NoteSearchResult: TypedDict with note_id: str, content: str, score: float. @lru_cache def get_note_search_service() -> NoteSearchService. Class NoteSearchService, method: async def search(query: str, k: int = 10, semantic: bool = True) -> list[NoteSearchResult]. If query.strip() == '': return []. FTS5 path: execute 'SELECT note_id, content, bm25(notes_fts) AS score FROM notes_fts WHERE notes_fts MATCH :q ORDER BY score LIMIT :k' with sanitized query. Semantic path (if semantic=True): encode query via get_embedding_service() in executor, search note_vectors table with top-k. Merge with RRF (score = 1/(60+rank) summed per note_id). Return deduplicated list sorted by merged score descending. NoteSearchService takes an AsyncSession in search() -- use get_session_factory() same pattern as other services. API -- GET /notes/search?q=...&k=10&semantic=true. Response: NoteSearchResponse(BaseModel) with results: list[NoteSearchResult model -- create NoteSearchResultResponse(BaseModel) with same fields]. Validate: if q is empty string return HTTP 422 with message 'q must be non-empty'. FRONTEND -- In Notes.tsx, add a search bar in the page header above the view toggle (controlled input, 300ms debounce). When debouncedQ is non-empty, switch to search mode: queryKey=['notes-search', debouncedQ], fetches GET /notes/search?q={debouncedQ}. Display results in existing list/card view. When input cleared, revert to normal GET /notes. Add X button to clear. Loading: skeleton rows. Error: inline message. Empty: 'No notes matching "query"'. DOCS -- Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to document notes_fts and note_vectors under Data Stores. TESTS -- backend/tests/test_note_search.py: (a) test_fts_finds_exact_match: insert note with content 'The White Rabbit led Alice into Wonderland'; search('White Rabbit', semantic=False); assert note_id in results. (b) test_fts_sync_on_delete: create note, verify FTS hit, delete note, verify no FTS hit. (c) test_fts_sync_on_update: create note content='original', update to 'updated text', search('updated text', semantic=False); assert hit; search('original', semantic=False); assert miss. (d) test_search_empty_query: search('', semantic=False); assert returns []. (e) test_search_api_empty_q: GET /notes/search?q=; assert HTTP 422. stories.title: Feature: Notes vs Book gap detection -- surface book concepts absent from notes
stories.description:
CONTEXT: A learner writes notes on a book but misses concepts. Knowing what the book covers that your notes do not is high-value feedback. This story adds a gap detection endpoint: given notes (by tag or IDs) and a book document, retrieve the most relevant book passages, then use LLM to identify concepts in the book absent from the notes. BACKEND -- TYPES: In backend/app/types.py add GapReport TypedDict: class GapReport(TypedDict): gaps: list[str]; covered: list[str]; query_used: str. Add at the bottom of types.py (read the file first to find a safe insertion point). SERVICE -- Create backend/app/services/gap_detector.py. Class GapDetectorService, @lru_cache def get_gap_detector() -> GapDetectorService. Method: async def detect_gaps(note_ids: list[str], document_id: str, k: int = 8, session: AsyncSession) -> GapReport. Implementation: (1) Fetch notes by note_ids via session. If none found, raise ValueError('No notes found for given IDs'). Combine content into notes_text (cap 3000 chars). (2) Use first 200 chars of notes_text as query_used. (3) Call get_retriever().retrieve(query=query_used, document_ids=[document_id], k=k, session=session) -- read retriever.py to confirm the exact signature before writing this call. (4) Build book_context from chunk texts (cap 3000 chars). (5) Call litellm.acompletion with system='You are a learning gap analyst. Given learner notes and book passages on the same topic, return JSON: {"gaps": ["..."], "covered": ["..."]}. Each item is one sentence max. Gaps are key concepts from the passages absent or barely mentioned in the notes. Covered are concepts the notes address well. Be specific -- name actual concepts.' User message: 'NOTES:
{notes_text}

BOOK PASSAGES:
{book_context}'. (6) Parse JSON; on malformed response return GapReport(gaps=['Gap analysis unavailable -- could not parse LLM response'], covered=[], query_used=query_used). (7) On litellm.ServiceUnavailableError: raise HTTPException(503, detail='Ollama is unreachable. Start it with: ollama serve'). Structured logging only. API -- In backend/app/routers/notes.py add POST /notes/gap-detect. Input: GapDetectRequest(BaseModel) with note_ids: list[str] = Field(min_length=1), document_id: str. Validate note_ids non-empty (422 if empty -- Field(min_length=1) handles this). Response: GapDetectResponse(BaseModel) with gaps: list[str], covered: list[str], query_used: str. Call get_gap_detector().detect_gaps(...). On ValueError from service (no notes found): HTTP 404. On HTTPException 503: propagate. FRONTEND -- In Notes.tsx, add 'Compare with Book' button in page header. On click open GapDetectDialog (new file: frontend/src/components/GapDetectDialog.tsx). Dialog: (a) document selector -- dropdown populated from GET /documents (read documents.py router to confirm the endpoint path and response schema -- the endpoint exists at GET /documents as a paginated list; use title field for display); (b) note multi-select list (same pattern as GenerateFlashcardsDialog from S93 -- consider extracting a shared NoteMultiSelect component if it simplifies both dialogs, otherwise duplicate is acceptable); (c) 'Analyze Gaps' button with inline Loader2 while pending (dialog stays open -- invariant 14). On success: show two sections -- 'Concepts the book covers that your notes miss' (gaps as bullets) and 'Concepts your notes cover well' (covered as bullets). If gaps is empty: 'Your notes cover this material well!'. On 503: 'Ollama is unavailable. Start it with: ollama serve' (invariant 18). Loading/error/empty states required (invariant 13). TESTS -- backend/tests/test_gap_detector.py: (a) test_detect_gaps_returns_report: create 1 note with content 'Alice meets the Cheshire Cat'; mock get_retriever().retrieve to return 3 fake ScoredChunk objects about Wonderland; mock litellm.acompletion to return valid JSON gap report; call detect_gaps([note_id], 'fake-doc-id', k=3, session); assert GapReport has non-empty gaps and covered lists. (b) test_detect_gaps_no_notes_found: call detect_gaps(['nonexistent-id'], 'doc-id', k=3, session); assert ValueError raised. (c) test_detect_gaps_malformed_llm: mock litellm to return non-JSON text; assert GapReport returned with gaps=['Gap analysis unavailable -- could not parse LLM response'] and covered=[]. (d) test_gap_detect_api_422_empty_note_ids: POST /notes/gap-detect with body '{"note_ids":[],"document_id":"abc"}'; assert HTTP 422. (e) test_gap_detect_api_503: mock get_gap_detector().detect_gaps to raise HTTPException(503); POST /notes/gap-detect; assert HTTP 503. (f) test_alice_gap_detection_slow (marked @pytest.mark.slow, uses all_books_ingested fixture from conftest_books.py): create a note 'Alice follows the White Rabbit and meets the Cheshire Cat'; call detect_gaps([note_id], alice_doc_id, k=5, session) with real Retriever (no mock) and LiteLLM mocked to return a valid JSON report; assert retrieve was called (check context contains book passage text) -- verify that book_context in the LLM call prompt contains at least one passage from the Alice corpus. stories.title: Inline Gap Analysis Card in Chat: notes-vs-book comparison via chat intent, establishes shared card SSE contract
stories.description:
CONTEXT: The GapDetectDialog (S94) is a multi-step modal that breaks learning flow. This story moves gap detection into the chat stream and -- critically -- establishes the shared '__card__' SSE protocol that S97, S98, and S99 all build on. Read this description carefully; the card contract defined here must not be changed in subsequent stories. SPAWN luminary-planner before writing any code: this story touches types.py, intent.py, chat_graph.py, qa.py (or wherever the SSE stream loop lives), Chat.tsx, GapResultCard.tsx, and Notes.tsx -- 7 files across 3 domains. THE __CARD__ SSE CONTRACT (foundational for S97-S101): any chat graph node that produces a structured card result (instead of streamed text) sets state['answer'] = '__card__' + json.dumps({...}). In the SSE stream loop: detect if state['answer'].startswith('__card__'), if yes emit exactly one SSE event: 'data: ' + json.dumps({'card': json.loads(state['answer'][8:])}) + '

', then emit the standard 'done' event. No 'token' events are emitted for card answers. On the frontend, the SSE parser detects a 'card' key in the payload and creates a Message with type='card' and cardData=payload['card'] instead of appending text. This protocol must be documented as a comment block at the top of the SSE loop so Ralph can find it in S97-S99. BACKEND -- TYPES: In backend/app/types.py line 41, extend IntentType Literal to add 'notes_gap'. Read line 41 before editing. INTENT HEURISTIC: In backend/app/services/intent.py, add a new rule BEFORE all existing rules (first-match wins): if any phrase in the lowercased query matches the set {'find gaps in my notes', 'gaps in my notes', 'what am i missing', 'what did i miss', 'compare my notes', 'my notes vs', 'notes against the book', 'notes versus the book', 'missing from my notes'}: return ('notes_gap', 0.95). NOTES_GAP_NODE: In backend/app/runtime/chat_graph.py, add async def notes_gap_node(state: ChatState) -> dict. (1) Get document_id: state['doc_ids'][0] if len(state.get('doc_ids', [])) == 1 else None. If None: return card with error='Please select a specific document to compare against your notes.' (2) Get async session via get_session_factory() -- grep for this pattern in chat_graph.py first. Query: SELECT id FROM notes WHERE document_id=:doc_id LIMIT 50. (3) If no rows: return card with error='No notes found for this document. Create some notes first.' (4) Call await get_gap_detector().detect_gaps(note_ids=[r.id for r in rows], document_id=document_id, k=8, session=session). Import get_gap_detector from app.services.gap_detector. (5) On litellm.ServiceUnavailableError: return card with error='Ollama is unreachable. Start it with: ollama serve'. (6) Return {'answer': '__card__' + json.dumps({'type': 'gap_result', 'gaps': report['gaps'], 'covered': report['covered'], 'document_id': document_id}), 'context_chunks': []}. ROUTING: In route_node add 'notes_gap' -> 'notes_gap_node'. notes_gap_node routes to END (no synthesize_node). Wire in build_chat_graph(). SSE STREAM: Implement the __card__ protocol described above in the SSE loop. Add the comment block explaining the protocol. NOTES.TSX: The 'Compare with Book' button currently opens GapDetectDialog. Replace its onClick with navigation to the Chat tab + pre-fill 'Find gaps in my notes'. Read Notes.tsx to find the current button and the tab-switching mechanism before editing. Do NOT delete GapDetectDialog.tsx -- leave it in place. FRONTEND -- CHAT.TSX MESSAGE INTERFACE: Extend the Message TypedDict/interface: add optional type?: 'text' | 'card' and cardData?: Record<string, unknown>. Default type='text' for all existing messages. SSE PARSER: In the SSE parsing loop (around line 202 -- read Chat.tsx first), add: if (typeof payload['card'] !== 'undefined') { create card-type message with cardData=payload['card']; continue; }. MESSAGE RENDERING: In the JSX message list render, add branch: if (msg.type === 'card' && msg.cardData?.type === 'gap_result') render GapResultCard. stories.title: One-Click Bridge: generate flashcards from gap card in chat with no forms
stories.description: CONTEXT: After S96, GapResultCard shows what the user is missing but the [Generate Flashcards] button is disabled. This story wires it to a new endpoint that generates one LLM-authored flashcard per gap with no additional dialogs. This endpoint is also used by S99 (TeachBackResultCard) and S100 (confusion banner), so its API contract must be stable. BACKEND -- ENDPOINT: In backend/app/routers/notes.py (read the file first to confirm size; if too large create backend/app/routers/flashcard_extras.py and register it in main.py), add POST /flashcards/from-gaps. Input: FromGapsRequest(BaseModel): gaps: list[str] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=20), document_id: str = '' (empty string allowed -- required by S100 where no document context exists; do not add min_length). Response: FromGapsResponse(BaseModel): created: int, flashcard_ids: list[str]. 422 when gaps is empty list. SERVICE: In backend/app/services/ find or create a flashcard generation service (grep for 'generate' in existing service files before creating a new one -- reuse if a method already exists). Method: async def generate_from_gaps(gaps: list[str], document_id: str, session: AsyncSession) -> tuple[int, list[str]]. Implementation: (1) Use asyncio.Semaphore(5) for bounded LLM concurrency -- same pattern as SectionSummarizerService. (2) For each gap: call litellm.acompletion with system='You are a flashcard author. Given a learning gap, generate one flashcard as JSON: {"front": "clear question", "back": "concise answer (1-3 sentences)"}. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.', user=gap. (3) Parse JSON response. On malformed JSON: log warning, skip that gap (do not raise). (4) On litellm.ServiceUnavailableError for any call: raise HTTPException(503, detail='Ollama is unreachable. Start it with: ollama serve'). (5) Read FlashcardModel in models.py before building rows -- note all required fields and FSRS default state. Grep for how existing flashcard create endpoints initialize FSRS fields (e.g. in study.py POST endpoint) and use the same values. Set deck='gaps', document_id=document_id (may be empty string), use uuid4() for id, datetime.now(datetime.UTC) for timestamps. (6) Commit all rows in one transaction. Return (count, ids). FRONTEND -- GapResultCard.tsx: replace the disabled placeholder button with a working [Generate Flashcards for these Gaps] button. Button state machine: 'idle' -> click -> 'loading' (Loader2 inline, button disabled) -> 'done' (show '{N} flashcards added to your deck!' text + a 'Go to Study' link that switches to the Study tab using the tab-switching mechanism from Notes.tsx) | 'error' (inline error text below the button + retry resets to idle). Call POST /flashcards/from-gaps with {gaps: props.gaps, document_id: props.documentId}. On 503: show 'Ollama is unavailable. Start it with: ollama serve' as inline error. Button remains disabled when props.gaps.length === 0. TESTS -- backend/tests/test_from_gaps.py: (a) test_generate_from_gaps_creates_flashcards: mock litellm to return valid JSON {front, back} for each of 2 gaps; call generate_from_gaps; assert 2 FlashcardModel rows with deck='gaps'. (b) test_generate_from_gaps_skips_malformed_llm: gap 1 valid JSON, gap 2 non-JSON; assert 1 row created, no exception. (c) test_generate_from_gaps_503: mock litellm raises ServiceUnavailableError; assert HTTPException(503) raised. (d) test_from_gaps_api_422_empty_gaps: POST /flashcards/from-gaps with {gaps:[], document_id:'x'}; assert HTTP 422. (e) test_from_gaps_api_empty_document_id_allowed: POST /flashcards/from-gaps with {gaps:['concept A'], document_id:''}; mock litellm; assert HTTP 200 and created=1. (f) test_from_gaps_api_success: mock litellm; POST with 2 gaps; assert HTTP 200, created=2. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S97.sh: POST http://localhost:8000/flashcards/from-gaps with body '{"gaps":[],"document_id":""}'; assert HTTP 422. Exit 0.
