stories.title: Socratic Quiz Mode: chat generates targeted recall questions from active document
stories.description:
CONTEXT: Retrieval practice -- being tested before re-reading -- is the highest-leverage learning technique in cognitive science. This story adds a 'socratic' intent: when the user asks to be quizzed, the chat generates one targeted recall question from the document and renders it as a QuizQuestionCard using the __card__ SSE protocol established in S96. Read S96's description to understand the __card__ protocol before implementing -- do not re-invent it, extend it. BACKEND -- TYPES: In backend/app/types.py line 41, extend IntentType Literal to add 'socratic'. Read the file before editing. INTENT HEURISTIC: In backend/app/services/intent.py, add rule before existing rules: if any phrase matches {'quiz me', 'test me', 'ask me a question', 'ask me questions', 'socratic mode', 'give me a question', 'question me', 'what should i know'}: return ('socratic', 0.95). SOCRATIC_NODE: In backend/app/runtime/chat_graph.py, add async def socratic_node(state: ChatState) -> dict. (1) document_id = state['doc_ids'][0] if state.get('doc_ids') else None. (2) Retrieve k=5 chunks via get_retriever(): if document_id use it as filter, else retrieve with broad query 'key concept important idea' across all documents. Read how search_node calls get_retriever() and use the same pattern. (3) Build prompt: system='You are a Socratic tutor. Given passages from a learning document, generate ONE targeted recall question testing a specific fact, name, or concept. Format your response as exactly two lines:
Q: {the question}
CONTEXT: {1-2 sentence answer from the passages}
Output nothing else.', user='Passages:
' + '
---
'.join(chunk.text for chunk in retrieved[:5]). (4) Call litellm.acompletion (not streaming -- collect full response). Parse: split on newlines; extract line starting with 'Q:' and line starting with 'CONTEXT:'. Strip prefixes. On parse failure use fallback: q_text='What are the main ideas in this material?', context_text='See the document content.' (5) On litellm.ServiceUnavailableError: return '__card__' + json.dumps({'type': 'quiz_question', 'question': 'Quiz unavailable', 'context_hint': '', 'document_id': document_id or '', 'error': 'Ollama is unreachable. Start it with: ollama serve'}). (6) Return {'answer': '__card__' + json.dumps({'type': 'quiz_question', 'question': q_text, 'context_hint': context_text, 'document_id': document_id or ''})}. ROUTING: route_node returns 'socratic_node' when intent='socratic'. socratic_node routes to END. Wire in build_chat_graph(). SSE: the existing __card__ protocol from S96 handles this automatically -- no changes to the SSE loop needed. FRONTEND -- SSE PARSER: in Chat.tsx card handling, add case: if cardData.type === 'quiz_question' render QuizQuestionCard. Create frontend/src/components/QuizQuestionCard.tsx. Props: question: string, contextHint: string, documentId: string, error?: string. Layout: a card with border-l-4 border-blue-500, a 'Quiz' chip (small badge) at top-left, question text in font-semibold. If error prop: show amber inline error. Below question: a Textarea (auto-expand, minRows=2, placeholder='Type your answer...'). A [Submit Answer] button (primary, full-width). ON SUBMIT: the card appends a new user message to the chat: 'My answer: {userAnswer}'. This is a normal chat submission -- it routes to 'factual' intent, and the AI responds with the book answer, naturally evaluating the response. After submit: textarea becomes disabled, button hidden, show 'Answer submitted -- see the response below' in muted text. The card itself remains visible (read-only) so the user can reference the question. ERROR STATE: if error prop, show amber error text instead of question and textarea. LOADING: standard chat typing indicator while the node runs (isStreaming message already handled by Chat.tsx -- no new UI). TESTS -- backend/tests/test_chat_graph_socratic.py: (a) test_socratic_intent_heuristic: classify_intent_heuristic('quiz me on this') == 'socratic'. (b) test_socratic_no_false_positive: classify_intent_heuristic('what is the main theme?') != 'socratic'. (c) test_socratic_node_returns_card: mock retriever and litellm returning 'Q: Who is Achilles?
CONTEXT: Achilles is the hero.'; run socratic_node; assert answer starts '__card__', parsed type='quiz_question', question='Who is Achilles?'. (d) test_socratic_parse_fallback: mock litellm returns non-parseable text; assert card returned with fallback question, no exception. (e) test_socratic_ollama_offline: mock ServiceUnavailableError; assert card with error field, no exception raised. (f) test_route_node_socratic: ChatState intent='socratic'; assert route_node returns 'socratic_node'. stories.title: Teach It Back Mode: user explains a concept, AI evaluates and surfaces misconceptions
stories.description:
CONTEXT: The protege effect -- learning by teaching -- is one of the most reliable techniques for consolidating understanding. When the user says 'let me explain X', the chat retrieves authoritative passages and evaluates their explanation precisely: confirming what is right, correcting what is wrong, naming gaps. This story uses the __card__ protocol from S96 and the POST /flashcards/from-gaps endpoint from S97 -- both must exist before implementing this story. Do not re-create either. BACKEND -- TYPES: In backend/app/types.py line 41, extend IntentType Literal to add 'teach_back'. Read before editing. INTENT HEURISTIC: In backend/app/services/intent.py add rule before existing rules: if any phrase matches {'let me explain', 'i think this means', 'i understand this as', 'my understanding is', 'in my own words', 'i believe that', 'if i understand correctly', 'here is my understanding'}: return ('teach_back', 0.95). IMPORTANT: 'explain X to me' (imperative, asking the AI to explain) must NOT match -- the heuristic checks that the user is doing the explaining. The phrases listed are all first-person declarations; imperative 'explain ...' alone does not match any of them. TEACH_BACK_NODE: In backend/app/runtime/chat_graph.py, add async def teach_back_node(state: ChatState) -> dict. (1) document_id = state['doc_ids'][0] if state.get('doc_ids') else None. (2) Use first 150 chars of state['query'] as retrieval query to find relevant chunks (the explanation usually contains the concept terms). Retrieve k=5 chunks via get_retriever() -- same pattern as search_node. (3) Build prompt: system='You are a learning coach. The learner explained a concept in their own words. Identify: (a) what they got right, (b) specific misconceptions (things stated that are factually wrong), (c) important gaps (key aspects not mentioned). Be specific -- name exact concepts. Do not re-explain the whole topic. Structure response as JSON: {"correct": ["..."], "misconceptions": ["..."], "gaps": ["..."], "encouragement": "one sentence of genuine encouragement"}', user='LEARNER EXPLANATION:
' + state['query'] + '

AUTHORITATIVE PASSAGES:
' + '
---
'.join(c.text for c in chunks[:5])[:3000]. (4) Call litellm.acompletion (not streaming). Parse JSON. On malformed JSON: return card with correct=[], misconceptions=[], gaps=[], encouragement='I had trouble analyzing your explanation. Try rephrasing.', error_detail='Could not parse evaluation'. On ServiceUnavailableError: return card with error='Ollama is unreachable. Start it with: ollama serve'. (5) Return {'answer': '__card__' + json.dumps({'type': 'teach_back_result', 'correct': [...], 'misconceptions': [...], 'gaps': [...], 'encouragement': '...', 'document_id': document_id or ''})}. ROUTING: route_node returns 'teach_back_node' when intent='teach_back'. teach_back_node routes to END. Wire in build_chat_graph(). SSE: handled by existing __card__ protocol from S96 -- no changes to SSE loop. FRONTEND -- SSE PARSER: add case in Chat.tsx card handler: if cardData.type === 'teach_back_result' render TeachBackResultCard. Create frontend/src/components/TeachBackResultCard.tsx. Props: correct: string[], misconceptions: string[], gaps: string[], encouragement: string, documentId: string, error?: string. Layout: encouragement at top in italic text-muted-foreground. Three conditional sections (only rendered if non-empty): 'What you got right' (green check bullets), 'Misconceptions to correct' (red X bullets), 'Gaps to fill' (amber warning bullets). If all three arrays are empty and no error: show 'Excellent explanation! Nothing to correct.' At bottom: [Add Gaps to My Flashcards] button -- disabled when gaps.length === 0. ON CLICK: call POST /flashcards/from-gaps (created in S97 -- do not recreate the endpoint) with body {gaps: props.gaps, document_id: props.documentId}. Button state machine: idle → loading (Loader2) → done ('{N} flashcards added. Go to Study ->') | error (inline + retry). Reuse the same fetch pattern from GapResultCard without extracting a shared hook (duplication is acceptable here). On 503: show 'Ollama is unavailable. Start it with: ollama serve'. ERROR PROP: if error key in card data, show amber inline error instead of sections. TESTS -- backend/tests/test_chat_graph_teach_back.py: (a) test_teach_back_intent_heuristic: classify_intent_heuristic('let me explain what I understand') == 'teach_back'. (b) test_teach_back_no_false_positive: classify_intent_heuristic('explain quantum entanglement to me') != 'teach_back'. (c) test_teach_back_node_returns_card: mock retriever and litellm (valid JSON with correct, misconceptions, gaps); run teach_back_node; assert answer starts '__card__', parsed type='teach_back_result'. (d) test_teach_back_malformed_json_fallback: mock litellm returns non-JSON; assert card returned with encouragement fallback, no exception. (e) test_teach_back_ollama_offline: mock ServiceUnavailableError; assert card with error field, no exception. (f) test_route_node_teach_back: ChatState intent='teach_back'; assert route_node returns 'teach_back_node'. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S99.sh: POST http://localhost:8000/qa/stream with body '{"question":"let me explain what I understand about this topic","document_ids":[],"scope":"all"}'; assert HTTP 200. Exit 0.

stories.title: Confusion Detection Nudge: repeated questions across sessions surface a flashcard suggestion
stories.description: CONTEXT: When a learner asks about the same concept multiple times across sessions, they have not consolidated that knowledge. The qa_history table records every question. This story analyzes that history on chat load (DB-only, no LLM) and shows a dismissible amber banner when a stubborn concept is detected. The banner's [Add to Flashcards] button uses POST /flashcards/from-gaps (created in S97 with document_id='' support) -- do not recreate that endpoint. BACKEND -- SERVICE: Create backend/app/services/confusion_detector.py. ConfusionSignal TypedDict: concept: str, count: int, last_asked: str (ISO datetime string). @lru_cache def get_confusion_detector() -> ConfusionDetectorService. Class ConfusionDetectorService, method: async def detect(session: AsyncSession, lookback_days: int = 30, threshold: int = 3) -> list[ConfusionSignal]. Implementation: (1) Cutoff = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(days=lookback_days). Query qa_history: SELECT question, created_at FROM qa_history WHERE created_at >= :cutoff ORDER BY created_at DESC. (2) Tokenize each question: lowercase, split on non-alphanumeric chars, remove stopwords. Stopwords are a module-level frozenset constant _STOPWORDS: {'what', 'is', 'the', 'a', 'an', 'how', 'does', 'who', 'where', 'when', 'why', 'do', 'did', 'are', 'was', 'were', 'which', 'in', 'on', 'at', 'to', 'for', 'of', 'and', 'or', 'not', 'i', 'me', 'my', 'this', 'that', 'it', 'its', 'with', 'from', 'by', 'about', 'can', 'tell', 'explain', 'describe'}. (3) Count term frequencies. Track most recent created_at per term. (4) Return top 3 terms with count >= threshold as ConfusionSignal list, ordered by count desc. If total rows < threshold: return []. ENDPOINT: Add GET /chat/confusion-signals to backend/app/routers/ -- grep main.py to find where chat/qa routers are registered and add there, or create backend/app/routers/chat_meta.py and register it. Response: list[ConfusionSignalResponse(BaseModel)]: concept: str, count: int, last_asked: str. Call get_confusion_detector().detect(session). DB-only, no LLM, must return in < 200ms. FRONTEND -- Chat.tsx: on component mount call GET /chat/confusion-signals via useQuery(queryKey=['confusion-signals'], staleTime: 300_000). If data is non-empty and data[0].count >= 3: show ConfusionBanner. CONFUSION BANNER: render as a div in Chat.tsx JSX above the messages list (no separate file needed). Styling: bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-950, border border-amber-200 dark:border-amber-800, rounded-lg p-3 mb-2, flex items-center justify-between. Left: text 'You have asked about "{concept}" {count} times. Add it to your flashcards?'. Right: two buttons: [Add to Flashcards] (size='sm', variant='outline') and [x] dismiss (size='icon', variant='ghost', Lucide X icon). DISMISS: useState<Set<string>> tracking dismissed concepts. Filter signals against this set before rendering. Do NOT use localStorage -- dismissed signals return on next page load (intentional: the nudge re-appears until the user acts). [ADD TO FLASHCARDS]: POST /flashcards/from-gaps with {gaps: [signal.concept], document_id: ''} (empty document_id is valid per S97). On success: add concept to dismissed set. On 503: show inline error text in the banner replacing the buttons temporarily; retry button resets to idle. Loading state for the add action: replace [Add to Flashcards] button with Loader2 while in flight. QUERY LOADING/ERROR: while confusion query is loading or errored: no banner (fail silently). TESTS -- backend/tests/test_confusion_detector.py: (a) test_detect_returns_signal_above_threshold: insert 4 qa_history rows within 30 days each containing 'entanglement'; detect(threshold=3); assert ConfusionSignal with concept='entanglement', count=4. (b) test_detect_below_threshold_returns_empty: 2 rows with 'entanglement'; assert []. (c) test_detect_ignores_stopwords: 4 rows where only repeated non-stopword is 'Alice'; assert 'Alice' found, no stopword in results. (d) test_detect_lookback_respected: 2 rows within 30 days, 3 rows older than 30 days (all 'quasar'); assert [] (not enough in window). (e) test_confusion_api_returns_list: GET /chat/confusion-signals; assert HTTP 200 and JSON array. SMOKE -- scripts/smoke/S100.sh: GET http://localhost:8000/chat/confusion-signals; assert HTTP 200 and JSON array response. Exit 0.

stories.title: Study Session Planner: pre-session agenda from FSRS state, gaps, and document progress
stories.description: CONTEXT: Learners open the app not knowing where to start. The data for a prioritized plan already exists: FSRS due cards (urgency), weak flashcard areas (gaps), and recently ingested documents (reading progress). This story adds GET /study/session-plan and renders the result as an inline slide-up panel in Chat triggered by a 4th pill added to S95's ChatSuggestions. This story MODIFIES ChatSuggestions from S95 -- read that component carefully before editing. BACKEND -- ENDPOINT: In backend/app/routers/study.py (read in full before editing), add GET /study/session-plan. Query param: minutes: int = Query(default=20, ge=5, le=120) -- 422 outside bounds. Response: SessionPlanResponse(BaseModel): total_minutes: int, items: list[SessionPlanItem]. SessionPlanItem(BaseModel): type: Literal['review', 'gap', 'read'], title: str, minutes: int, action_label: str, action_target: str.
